title: Using gtscales
text: work, gtscale_data_color_*() helpers fastest path. call gt::data_color() attach matching legend. Binned scales useful want fixed intervals rather smooth gradient. Quantile scales instead split data equally sized groups. Discrete legends useful colors encode categories rather ordered values.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_continuous(     column = num,     palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),     labels = big_number_labels,     width = '220px',     title = 'Numeric scale'   ) exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_bins(     column = currency,     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000, 70000),     title = 'Currency bins'   ) exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_quantiles(     column = num,     palette = c('#fdd49e', '#fdbb84', '#ef6548', '#990000'),     quantiles = 4,     labels = big_number_labels,     width = '220px',     title = 'Quartiles'   ) data.frame(   district = c('A', 'B', 'C', 'D'),   status = c('Safe D', 'Toss-up', 'Lean R', 'Safe R'),   margin = c(18, 2, -6, -21) ) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_discrete(     column = status,     values = c('#2166ac', '#f7f7f7', '#f4a3b4', '#b2182b'),     labels = c('Safe D', 'Toss-up', 'Lean R', 'Safe R'),     title = 'Race rating'   )

title: Using gtscales
text: Sometimes table already colored, color mapping handled elsewhere. case, gtscale_color_*() helpers attach legend.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gt::data_color(     columns = num,     method = 'numeric',     palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1')   ) |>   gtscale_color_continuous(     column = num,     palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),     title = 'Numeric scale'   )

title: Using gtscales
text: control, use gtscale_spec. Specs separate scale definition application legend placement. becomes useful scale needs reused want separate coloring legend placement.
code: spec <- gtscale_spec_continuous(   num,   palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),   labels = big_number_labels,   width = '220px',   title = 'Numeric scale' ) |>   gtscale_spec_set_application(apply_to = 'fill') |>   gtscale_spec_set_legend(placement = 'subtitle')  exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_apply_legend(spec)

title: Using gtscales
text: gtscales designed accept kinds helpers already use plots.

title: Using gtscales
text: can pass label functions break functions directly.
code: data.frame(share = c(0.12, 0.33, 0.57, 0.91)) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_bins(     column = share,     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1),     labels = label_percent(),     title = 'Share bins'   )

title: Using gtscales
text: Palette functions scales can supplied directly.
code: data.frame(value = c(1, 10, 100, 1000)) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_continuous(     column = value,     palette = pal_viridis(),     transform = 'log10',     breaks = breaks_log(),     labels = label_number(),     title = 'Log scale'   )

title: Using gtscales
text: Date-like columns work continuous binned workflows. many cases, gtscales can infer appropriate transform column class.
code: data.frame(   when = as.Date(c('2024-01-01', '2024-01-20', '2024-02-10', '2024-03-05')),   value = c(10, 18, 35, 52) ) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_bins(     column = when,     palette = pal_viridis(),     bins = breaks_width('1 month'),     labels = date_labels,     width = '220px',     title = 'Monthly bins'   )

title: Using gtscales
text: Legends can attached source notes, subtitles, titles. Source notes default portable across outputs. want legend closer heading, use subtitle title. attach multiple legends heading area, use layout = "inline" place side side.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_legend(     gtscale_spec_continuous(       num,       palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),       labels = big_number_labels,       width = '220px',       title = 'Numeric scale'     ) |>       gtscale_spec_set_legend(placement = 'source_note')   ) exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_legend(     gtscale_spec_quantiles(       num,       palette = c('#fdd49e', '#fdbb84', '#ef6548', '#990000'),       quantiles = 4,       labels = big_number_labels,       width = '220px',       title = 'Quartiles'     ) |>       gtscale_spec_set_legend(placement = 'subtitle')   ) exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_legend(     gtscale_spec_continuous(       num,       palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),       labels = big_number_labels,       width = '220px',       title = 'Numeric scale'     ) |>       gtscale_spec_set_legend(placement = 'title')   ) data.frame(a = 1:3, b = 4:6) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_legend(     gtscale_spec_continuous(       a,       palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),       title = 'A'     ) |>       gtscale_spec_set_legend(placement = 'subtitle', layout = 'inline')   ) |>   gtscale_legend(     gtscale_spec_continuous(       b,       palette = c('#fff5eb', '#7f2704'),       title = 'B'     ) |>       gtscale_spec_set_legend(placement = 'subtitle', layout = 'inline')   )

title: Using gtscales
text: Validated example workflows live inst/examples : HTML PDF/LaTeX Typst DOCX RTF gt integration strongest legends attached source notes, since path now works across HTML, LaTeX, DOCX, RTF. Heading placement still useful, best reserved workflows control output path tightly.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Christopher T. Kenny. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kenny C (2026). gtscales: Color Scale Extensions 'gt'. R package version 0.0.1, https://christophertkenny.com/gtscales/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt'},   author = {Christopher T. Kenny},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://christophertkenny.com/gtscales/}, }

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: goal gtscales make color-encoded gt tables easier read adding matched legends directly rendered output.

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: can install development version gtscales GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("christopherkenny/gtscales")

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_continuous() colors column adds matching gradient legend.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_continuous(     column = num,     palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),     labels = big_number_labels,     width = '220px',     title = 'Numeric scale'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_bins() useful color mapping interval-based.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_bins(     column = currency,     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000, 70000),     title = 'Currency bins'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_discrete() useful colors encode compact status class variable benefits legend.
code: data.frame(   district = c('A', 'B', 'C', 'D'),   status = c('Safe D', 'Toss-up', 'Lean R', 'Safe R'),   margin = c(18, 2, -6, -21) ) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_discrete(     column = status,     values = c('#2166ac', '#f7f7f7', '#f4a3b4', '#b2182b'),     labels = c('Safe D', 'Toss-up', 'Lean R', 'Safe R'),     title = 'Race rating'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscale_data_color_quantiles() useful want evenly sized rank groups instead fixed numeric cutpoints.
code: exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_quantiles(     column = num,     palette = c('#fdd49e', '#fdbb84', '#ef6548', '#990000'),     quantiles = 4,     labels = big_number_labels,     width = '220px',     title = 'Quartiles'   )

title: Color Scale Extensions for gt
text: gtscales designed accept kinds helpers already use scales. can pass label functions, break functions, transform specifications, palette functions directly.
code: data.frame(   when = as.Date(c('2024-01-01', '2024-01-20', '2024-02-10', '2024-03-05')),   value = c(10, 18, 35, 52) ) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_bins(     column = when,     palette = scales::pal_viridis(),     bins = scales::breaks_width('1 month'),     labels = date_labels,     width = '220px',     title = 'Monthly bins'   )  data.frame(value = c(1, 10, 100, 1000)) |>   gt() |>   gtscale_data_color_continuous(     column = value,     palette = scales::pal_viridis(),     transform = 'log10',     breaks = scales::breaks_log(),     labels = big_number_labels,     width = '220px'   )

title: Apply a gtscales spec and attach its legend — gtscale_apply_legend
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Apply a gtscales spec and attach its legend — gtscale_apply_legend
code: library(gt)  spec <- gtscale_spec_continuous(   num,   palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),   title = 'Value' )  exibble |>   gt() |>   gtscale_apply_legend(spec)     num       char       fctr       date       time       datetime       currency       row       group     1.111e-01 apricot one 2015-01-15 13:35 2018-01-01 02:22 49.950 row_1 grp_a2.222e+00 banana two 2015-02-15 14:40 2018-02-02 14:33 17.950 row_2 grp_a3.333e+01 coconut three 2015-03-15 15:45 2018-03-03 03:44 1.390 row_3 grp_a4.444e+02 durian four 2015-04-15 16:50 2018-04-04 15:55 65100.000 row_4 grp_a5.550e+03 NA five 2015-05-15 17:55 2018-05-05 04:00 1325.810 row_5 grp_bNA fig six 2015-06-15 NA 2018-06-06 16:11 13.255 row_6 grp_b7.770e+05 grapefruit seven NA 19:10 2018-07-07 05:22 NA row_7 grp_b8.880e+06 honeydew eight 2015-08-15 20:20 NA 0.440 row_8 grp_bValue2,000,0004,000,0006,000,0008,000,000

what: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
text: lower-level helper cases table coloring already handled elsewhere. usual "color legendize" workflow, prefer gtscale_data_color_bins().

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
code: gtscale_color_bins(   data,   column = NULL,   palette,   domain = NULL,   bins = NULL,   transform = NULL,   oob = NULL,   right = FALSE,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   width = "180px",   height = "14px" )

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column optional numeric, Date, POSIXt, difftime column underlying table used infer domain. palette vector colors, palette endpoints, single named palette, palette function used color bins. domain vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. bins Optional bin boundaries break function. omitted, breaks generated domain, column, transform. transform transformation specification understood scales::.transform(). used generating default bins interpreting break functions. oob --bounds handling function shortcut. Use function like scales::oob_squish() shortcut "censor" "squish". right Whether intervals closed right. default FALSE yields intervals like [, b). labels optional labeling function character vector bins. function supplied, applied bin boundaries interval labels constructed. title Optional legend title. width Width legend. height Height swatches.

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Add only a binned color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_bins
code: library(gt)  exibble |>   gt() |>   data_color(     columns = currency,     method = 'bin',     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000000)   ) |>   gtscale_color_bins(     column = currency,     palette = c('#f7fbff', '#08306b'),     bins = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000000),     title = 'Binned values'   )     num       char       fctr       date       time       datetime       currency       row       group     1.111e-01 apricot one 2015-01-15 13:35 2018-01-01 02:22 49.950 row_1 grp_a2.222e+00 banana two 2015-02-15 14:40 2018-02-02 14:33 17.950 row_2 grp_a3.333e+01 coconut three 2015-03-15 15:45 2018-03-03 03:44 1.390 row_3 grp_a4.444e+02 durian four 2015-04-15 16:50 2018-04-04 15:55 65100.000 row_4 grp_a5.550e+03 NA five 2015-05-15 17:55 2018-05-05 04:00 1325.810 row_5 grp_bNA fig six 2015-06-15 NA 2018-06-06 16:11 13.255 row_6 grp_b7.770e+05 grapefruit seven NA 19:10 2018-07-07 05:22 NA row_7 grp_b8.880e+06 honeydew eight 2015-08-15 20:20 NA 0.440 row_8 grp_bBinned values0.4 - 1010.0 - 100100.0 - 1,0001,000.0 - 65,100

what: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
text: lower-level helper cases table coloring already handled elsewhere. usual "color legendize" workflow, prefer gtscale_data_color_continuous().

title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
code: gtscale_color_continuous(   data,   column = NULL,   palette = NULL,   domain = NULL,   breaks = NULL,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   transform = NULL,   oob = NULL,   direction = "to right",   width = "160px",   height = "14px",   fn = NULL )

title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column optional numeric column underlying table used infer domain. palette vector colors used legend gradient. single named palette palette function can also supplied. domain numeric vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. breaks Optional break values break function display gradient. labels optional labeling function character vector breaks. title Optional legend title. transform transformation specification understood scales::.transform(). omitted, appropriate transform inferred data. oob --bounds handling function shortcut. Use function like scales::oob_squish() shortcut "censor", "squish", "keep", "discard". direction CSS gradient direction. Defaults "right". width Width legend bar. height Height legend bar. fn Backward-compatible fallback passing scales palette function. palette preferred.

title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Add only a continuous color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_continuous
code: library(gt)  exibble |>   gt() |>   data_color(     columns = num,     method = 'numeric',     palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1')   ) |>   gtscale_color_continuous(     column = num,     palette = c('#A0442C', 'white', '#0063B1'),     title = 'Value'   )     num       char       fctr       date       time       datetime       currency       row       group     1.111e-01 apricot one 2015-01-15 13:35 2018-01-01 02:22 49.950 row_1 grp_a2.222e+00 banana two 2015-02-15 14:40 2018-02-02 14:33 17.950 row_2 grp_a3.333e+01 coconut three 2015-03-15 15:45 2018-03-03 03:44 1.390 row_3 grp_a4.444e+02 durian four 2015-04-15 16:50 2018-04-04 15:55 65100.000 row_4 grp_a5.550e+03 NA five 2015-05-15 17:55 2018-05-05 04:00 1325.810 row_5 grp_bNA fig six 2015-06-15 NA 2018-06-06 16:11 13.255 row_6 grp_b7.770e+05 grapefruit seven NA 19:10 2018-07-07 05:22 NA row_7 grp_b8.880e+06 honeydew eight 2015-08-15 20:20 NA 0.440 row_8 grp_bValue2,000,0004,000,0006,000,0008,000,000

what: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete
title: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete
text: lower-level helper cases table coloring already handled elsewhere. usual "color legendize" workflow, prefer gtscale_data_color_discrete().

title: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete
code: gtscale_color_discrete(   data,   values,   labels = values,   title = NULL,   swatch_size = "12px" )

title: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). values vector color values single named discrete palette. labels Labels color swatch. Defaults values. title Optional legend title. swatch_size Size discrete color swatch.

title: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Add only a discrete color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_discrete
code: library(gt)  data.frame(   category = c('Low', 'Medium', 'High'),   value = c(12, 47, 83) ) |>   gt() |>   data_color(     columns = category,     method = 'factor',     palette = c('#1b9e77', '#d95f02', '#7570b3')   ) |>   gtscale_color_discrete(     values = c('#1b9e77', '#d95f02', '#7570b3'),     labels = c('Low', 'Medium', 'High'),     title = 'Category'   )     category       value     Low 12Medium 47High 83CategoryLowMediumHigh

what: Add only a diverging color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_diverging
title: Add only a diverging color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_diverging
text: lower-level helper cases table coloring already handled elsewhere. usual "color legendize" workflow, prefer gtscale_data_color_diverging().

title: Add only a diverging color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_diverging
code: gtscale_color_diverging(   data,   column = NULL,   palette,   domain = NULL,   midpoint = 0,   breaks = NULL,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   transform = NULL,   oob = NULL,   direction = "to right",   width = "160px",   height = "14px",   mid_color = "#FFFFFF" )

title: Add only a diverging color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_diverging
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column optional numeric column shared set numeric columns underlying table used infer domain. palette Two endpoint colors, three diverging colors, single named palette. domain numeric vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. midpoint Numeric midpoint used anchor diverging scale. breaks Optional break values break function display gradient. labels optional labeling function character vector breaks. title Optional legend title. transform transformation specification understood scales::.transform(). omitted, appropriate transform inferred data. oob --bounds handling function shortcut. Use function like scales::oob_squish() shortcut "censor", "squish", "keep", "discard". direction CSS gradient direction. Defaults "right". width Width legend bar. height Height legend bar. mid_color Midpoint color palette supplies two endpoint colors.

title: Add only a diverging color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_diverging
text: modified gt::gt table.

title: Add only a quantile color legend to a gt table — gtscale_color_quantiles
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column numeric, Date, POSIXt, difftime column underlying table used infer quantile boundaries. palette vector colors, palette endpoints, single named palette, palette function used color quantile bins. quantiles number quantile groups. oob --bounds handling function shortcut. Use function like scales::oob_squish() shortcut "censor" "squish". right Whether intervals closed right. default FALSE yields intervals like [, b). labels optional labeling function character vector quantile ranges. function supplied, applied quantile boundaries interval labels constructed. title Optional legend title. width Width legend. height Height swatches.

what: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins
title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins
text: primary interface binned scales gtscales.

title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins
code: gtscale_data_color_bins(   data,   column,   palette,   bins = NULL,   domain = NULL,   transform = NULL,   oob = NULL,   right = FALSE,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   width = "180px",   height = "14px",   apply_to = c("fill", "text"),   na_color = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   reverse = FALSE,   autocolor_text = TRUE,   contrast_algo = c("apca", "wcag"),   autocolor_light = "#FFFFFF",   autocolor_dark = "#000000" )

title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column numeric, Date, POSIXt, difftime column color legendize. palette vector colors, palette endpoints, single named palette, palette function used bins. bins Optional bin boundaries break function. omitted, breaks generated domain, column, transform. domain vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. transform transformation specification understood scales::.transform(). used generating default bins interpreting break functions. oob --bounds handling function shortcut. Use function like scales::oob_squish() shortcut "censor" "squish". right Whether intervals closed right. default FALSE yields intervals like [, b). labels optional labeling function character vector bins. function supplied, applied bin boundaries interval labels constructed. title Optional legend title. width Width legend. height Height swatches. apply_to Whether colors applied cell fill text. na_color Color used missing values. alpha Alpha applied gt::data_color(). reverse Whether reverse color mapping. autocolor_text Whether automatically adjust text color. contrast_algo Contrast algorithm passed gt::data_color(). autocolor_light Light text color used gt::data_color(). autocolor_dark Dark text color used gt::data_color().

title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching binned legend — gtscale_data_color_bins

code: gtscale_data_color_continuous(   data,   column,   palette = NULL,   domain = NULL,   breaks = NULL,   labels = NULL,   title = NULL,   transform = NULL,   oob = NULL,   direction = "to right",   width = "160px",   height = "14px",   apply_to = c("fill", "text"),   na_color = NULL,   alpha = NULL,   reverse = FALSE,   autocolor_text = TRUE,   contrast_algo = c("apca", "wcag"),   autocolor_light = "#FFFFFF",   autocolor_dark = "#000000",   fn = NULL )

title: Color a numeric gt column and add a matching continuous legend — gtscale_data_color_continuous
text: data gt_tbl created gt::gt(). column numeric column color legendize. palette vector colors used table legend gradient. single named palette palette function can also supplied. domain numeric vector length 2 giving scale limits. omitted, limits inferred column. breaks Optional break values break function display gradient. labels optional labeling function character vector breaks. title Optional legend title. transform transformation specification understood scales::.transform(). omitted, appropriate transform inferred data. oob --bounds handling function shortcut. Use function like scales::oob_squish() shortcut "censor", "squish", "keep", "discard". direction CSS gradient direction. Defaults "right". width Width legend bar. height Height legend bar. apply_to Whether colors applied cell fill text. na_color Color used missing values. alpha Alpha applied gt::data_color(). reverse Whether reverse color mapping. autocolor_text Whether automatically adjust text color. contrast_algo Contrast algorithm passed gt::data_color(). autocolor_light Light text color used gt::data_color(). autocolor_dark Dark text color used gt::data_color(). fn Optional scales function passed gt::data_color(). legend, palette preferred reliable inspecting closure internals.

code: spec <- gtscale_spec_quantiles(   num,   palette = c('#fdd49e', '#fdbb84', '#ef6548', '#990000'),   quantiles = 4,   title = 'Quartiles' )  gtscale_render_legend(   spec = spec,   data = gt::gt(gt::exibble),   output = 'latex' ) #> [1] "{\\raggedright \\textbf{Quartiles}\\\\\\textcolor[HTML]{FDD49E}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 0 - 18\\quad \\textcolor[HTML]{FDBB84}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 18 - 444\\quad \\textcolor[HTML]{EF6548}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 444 - 391,275\\quad \\textcolor[HTML]{990000}{\\rule{1.4em}{0.9ex}}\\ 391,275 - 8,880,000}"

text: Set gtscales legend rendered

title: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
code: gtscale_spec_set_legend(   spec,   output = "contextual",   placement = "source_note",   layout = c("stack", "inline"),   align = c("left", "center", "right"),   show_border = TRUE,   border_color = "#D0D7DE",   border_radius = "8px",   show_na = FALSE,   na_label = "Missing",   na_color = NULL )

title: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
text: spec gtscale_spec. output Output target legend. Use "contextual" gt-managed HTML/LaTeX source notes, choose specific output like "html", "latex", "typst". placement Legend placement target. "source_note", "title", "subtitle" currently implemented. layout Whether multiple legends heading area stack vertically sit inline. align Horizontal alignment legend container. show_border Whether legend bar, bin frame, swatches draw borders. border_color Border color used legend frames swatches. border_radius Border radius used HTML Typst legend frames. show_na Whether include explicit missing-value legend entry. na_label Label use missing values legend. na_color Optional legend swatch color missing values. omitted show_na = TRUE, neutral gray swatch used.

title: Set how a gtscales legend should be rendered — gtscale_spec_set_legend
text: modified gtscale_spec.

what: gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt' — gtscales-package
title: gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt' — gtscales-package
text: Adds legend helpers color-encoded 'gt' tables. primary interface combines 'gt::data_color()' matched legends continuous, binned, quantile, discrete scales, lower-level helpers support legend-reusable scale specification workflows.

title: gtscales: Color Scale Extensions for 'gt' — gtscales-package
text: Maintainer: Christopher T. Kenny ctkenny@proton.(ORCID)

title: gtscales 0.0.1
text: Initial development release gtscales, focused matched legends color-encoded gt tables. gtscale_data_color_*() gtscale_color_*() support continuous, binned, quantile, discrete, diverging midpoint-aware scales. gtscale_spec_*(), gtscale_apply(), gtscale_legend(), gtscale_apply_legend() support reusable scale definitions, shared scales across multiple columns, custom legend workflows. Named palette shortcuts recognized automatically grDevices::hcl.colors() grDevices::palette.colors(), including palettes "viridis", "Blues 3", "Okabe-Ito". Legends can placed source notes subtitles can include explicit missing-value entries.
